seoTitle: Lawn Hill vs Lawnton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lawn Hill and Lawnton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lawn Hill vs Lawnton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lawn Hill and Lawnton.
heading: Lawn Hill vs Lawnton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lawn Hill, Queensland and Lawnton, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lawn Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Lawnton (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lawn Hill or Lawnton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lawn Hill has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Lawnton scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lawn Hill scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Lawnton scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lawn Hill or Lawnton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lawn Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.53% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
